based_on: Byte-precise mutation of fixtures/mazur.golden.jsonl (engine-emitted canonical bytes). The bad fixture differs from the golden in exactly two places: the fixture_status block (3 fields) and the single targeted numeric value.

what_was_NOT_mutated: backward.hidden_error_signals.h1.downstream_contributions[0].downstream_signal (-0.138498562 — referenced by Rule 2 as the left factor)
backward.hidden_error_signals.h1.downstream_contributions[0].weight_value (0.40 — referenced by Rule 2 as the right factor)
backward.hidden_error_signals.h1.backpropagated_sum (-0.0363503064 — this is the sum claimed by the receipt; Rule 2's sum check will see the mutated contribution and report a sum mismatch as well)

single_target_invariant: Reconciliation produces at least one Rule 2 failure whose field_path names backward.hidden_error_signals.h1.downstream_contributions[0].value. A second Rule 2 failure on backward.hidden_error_signals.h1.backpropagated_sum is also expected and acceptable — when one contribution's stored value is wrong, the stored backprop_sum (computed from the original contributions) no longer equals sum(stored contributions), so the sum check also fires. Both are Rule 2 failures on the same unit h1, expressing the same root cause.
cascading_failures_observed: Rule 2 on backward.hidden_error_signals.h1.backpropagated_sum: sum(stored contributions) no longer matches stored backprop_sum, because contributions[0].value was mutated
failure_priority_assertion: Per docs/reconciliation.md, reconciliation failures report before fixture-lifecycle failures. The fixture_status.verification_state = 'expected_to_fail_reconciliation' on this fixture must NOT mask the Rule 2 failure.
purpose: Anti-circularity (Csmith / CompCert lineage): proves the reconciler checks Rule 2 per-contribution (downstream_signal × weight_value consistency) by detecting a 1e-6 mismatch (1000x the 1e-9 tolerance) between stored contribution.value and the product of (unmutated) downstream_signal × weight_value.
